req.title: Search patients
req.statement: A clinician shall search patients by name, MRN, or DOB; only patients in their care shall be returned.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-013 has three distinct testable behaviours: (1) a clinician receives only their own patients when searching by a valid parameter (EP positive), (2) a malformed query returns HTTP 400 (NEG negative), (3) a search term at the exact minimum accepted length (1 character) still produces a valid filtered response (BVA boundary). A fourth DT case covers the cross-product of search-parameter types to confirm each field (name/MRN/DOB) routes correctly and enforces care-team scoping. A fifth STC case validates that the audit log entry is written after a successful search, confirming the state transition from 'searched' to 'access-logged'.

tests.name: test_search_by_name_returns_only_assigned_patients
preconditions: At least one patient assigned to the clinician with family name 'Smith'
At least one patient NOT assigned to the clinician also named 'Smith' exists in the system
actions: GET /Patient?search with query param name=Smith using clinician bearer token
expected: Response body is a FHIR Bundle
All entries in the bundle belong to the clinician's care list
No patient outside the clinician's care is included
trace: R-HLT-013: only patients in their care shall be returned

tests.name: test_malformed_query_returns_400
actions: GET /Patient?search with an invalid query string (e.g. '%%bad%%') that violates the API contract
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error descriptor
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_malformed_query_returns_400(
    clinician_auth_fixture, base_url
):
    token = clinician_auth_fixture["token"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        params={"search": "%%bad%%"},
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    body = resp.json()
    has_error = (
        body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
        or "error" in body
        or "message" in body
    )
    assert has_error, "400 response must carry an error descriptor"

tests.name: test_single_char_name_search_is_accepted
preconditions: System accepts a minimum name search term of exactly 1 character
actions: GET /Patient?search with query param name=S (single character) using clinician bearer token
expected: Response body is a FHIR Bundle (may have zero entries)
All returned patients belong to the clinician's care list
trace: R-HLT-013: 400 when query malformed (boundary: minimum valid input must NOT trigger 400)

tests.name: test_search_by_mrn_and_dob_return_correct_patients
preconditions: Assigned patient with known MRN '123456' exists in care list
Assigned patient with DOB '1980-01-15' exists in care list
expected: MRN search returns bundle containing the patient with MRN 123456
DOB search returns bundle containing the patient born on 1980-01-15
No out-of-care-list patients appear in either response
trace: R-HLT-013: search patients by name, MRN, or DOB
R-HLT-013: only patients in their care shall be returned
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

@pytest.mark.parametrize("param,value,field_path", [
    ("mrn", "123456", ("identifier", "value")),
    ("dob", "1980-01-15", ("birthDate", None)),
])
def test_search_by_mrn_and_dob_return_correct_patients(
    clinician_auth_fixture, assigned_patient_ids, base_url,
    param, value, field_path
):
    token = clinician_auth_fixture["token"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        params={"search": f"{param}={value}"},
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("resourceType") == "Bundle"
    assert bundle.get("entry"), "Expected at least one match"
    for entry in bundle["entry"]:
        pid = entry["resource"]["id"]
        assert pid in assigned_patient_ids

tests.name: test_successful_search_creates_audit_log_entry
preconditions: Audit log endpoint or fixture <audit_log_fixture> accessible
At least one patient assigned to clinician
actions: Record current audit log count or last entry timestamp
GET /Patient?search with query param name=Smith using clinician bearer token
Query audit log for entries generated after the search
expected: A new audit log entry exists referencing the clinician's ID and the /Patient search action
State transitions from pre-search (no log entry) to post-search (log entry present)
trace: R-HLT-013: expected outcomes: filtered bundle returned; access logged
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_successful_search_creates_audit_log_entry(
    clinician_auth_fixture, audit_log_fixture, base_url
):
    token = clinician_auth_fixture["token"]
    clinician_id = clinician_auth_fixture["user_id"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    before_entries = audit_log_fixture.fetch_recent(clinician_id)
    before_count = len(before_entries)

    resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        params={"search": "name=Smith"},
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    after_entries = audit_log_fixture.fetch_recent(clinician_id)
    new_entries = [e for e in after_entries if e not in before_entries]
    assert len(new_entries) >= 1, "Audit log must record the search action"
    actions = [e.get("action") for e in new_entries]
    assert any("Patient" in a for a in actions), (
        "Audit entry must reference the Patient search"
    )
